title: senecio flaccidus var. durangensis
intro: Senecio flaccidus var. durangensis, also known as the thread-leaf greasewood or greasewood, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's leaves and stems contain a range of compounds that provide health benefits, including antiseptic, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin issues. Senecio flaccidus var. durangensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
